title: 10 best herbal teas for strep throat

remedy_properties: Glycyrrhizin: This compound has antibacterial properties, which can help combat the Streptococcus pyogenes bacteria that cause strep throat.

Flavonoids and phenolic acids: These bioactive compounds have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce swelling and pain in the throat and alleviate symptoms such as soreness and discomfort.
Liquiritin and isoliquiritigenin: These compounds have been shown to have antimicrobial activity against Streptococcus pyogenes, making them effective against strep throat infections.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra, also known as licorice, teas helps with strep throat because of its anti-inflammatory properties and soothing effects on the throat. The herb contains compounds that reduce swelling and pain, providing relief from the discomfort associated with strep throat. Additionally, licorice root has antimicrobial properties that help combat the underlying bacterial infection, allowing the body to heal and recover more efficiently. This makes licorice root tea a popular natural remedy for soothing and treating strep throat symptoms.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help reduce inflammation and alleviate the symptoms of strep throat, such as swelling and pain in the throat.

Saponins: Specifically, glycyrrhizin, a triterpenoid saponin, has been shown to have antibacterial properties, which help combat the bacterial infection that causes strep throat, and also exhibits anti-inflammatory effects to reduce throat irritation.

Liquiritin: This flavonoid glycoside has been reported to have a potent anti-inflammatory effect, which helps reduce the swelling and pain associated with strep throat, and also has antimicrobial properties that help combat the bacterial infection.
remedy_parts: Roots: They are used to make teas due to their high concentration of glycyrrhizin, a compound with anti-inflammatory properties that helps soothe sore throats.
Leaves: The leaves are used to make teas that help reduce inflammation and kill bacteria, making them effective against strep throat.
Barks: The barks of Glycyrrhiza glabra are used to make teas that have antimicrobial properties, helping to combat the underlying bacterial infection of strep throat.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 3-4 ounces of dried roots of glycyrrhiza glabra in late summer or early fall.
Rinse the dried roots with cold water to remove any impurities or dust particles.
Combine 1 teaspoon of the dried roots with 8 ounces of boiling water in a teapot.
Steep the mixture for 5-7 minutes allowing the roots to release their active compounds.
Strain the tea into a cup and let it cool before consumption for maximum benefit.

remedy_properties: Antimicrobial compounds: Kansas Coneflower tea contains antimicrobial compounds like echinacin, which help combat bacterial and viral infections, including streptococcus pyogenes, the bacteria responsible for strep throat.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The flavonoids and phenolic acids in Kansas Coneflower tea have anti-inflammatory effects, reducing swelling and pain associated with strep throat inflammation and infection.

Immune system support: Kansas Coneflower tea is believed to stimulate the immune system's natural response, enhancing the body's ability to fight off infections like strep throat by increasing white blood cell production and activity.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Echinacea angustifolia, also known as Kansas coneflower, teas helps with strep throat because of its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The root of the plant contains compounds like alkylamides and caffeic acid, which have been shown to reduce swelling and fight off bacterial infections. Studies have demonstrated that Echinacea angustifolia can inhibit the growth of Streptococcus pyogenes, the bacteria responsible for strep throat. By promoting immune function and reducing bacterial load, Echinacea angustifolia teas can help alleviate symptoms and support the body's natural recovery process.
remedy_constituents: Iridoid glycosides: These compounds help to reduce inflammation and promote healing in the throat, which can help alleviate symptoms of strep throat.
Alkylamides: Alkylamides have been shown to have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce swelling and pain in the throat associated with strep throat.
Cichoric acid: This phenolic compound has been found to have antimicrobial properties, which can help combat the bacteria that cause strep throat, such as Group A Streptococcus.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Echinacea angustifolia are the most commonly used part for making teas due to their high concentration of bioactive compounds that help boost the immune system.
Leaves: The leaves are also used to make teas as they contain some amount of the plant's bioactive compounds, although in lesser concentration than the roots.
Flowers: The flowers are used to make teas, especially in herbal blends, due to their potential anti-inflammatory properties and mild flavor.
remedy_recipe: Harvest echinacea angustifolia roots in the fall when the plant is dormant, typically after 2 years of growth.
Clean the roots thoroughly, rinse them with water, and dry them in a well-ventilated area.
Chop the dried roots into small pieces and store them in airtight containers to preserve their potency.
Steep 1-2 teaspoons of chopped echinacea angustifolia root in boiling water for 5-10 minutes.
Strain the tea and discard the solids, then serve the echinacea angustifolia tea hot or chilled as desired.

remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: Sage tea has antimicrobial properties that can help combat bacterial infections like strep throat by inhibiting the growth and reproduction of harmful bacteria.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds in sage tea can reduce inflammation and swelling in the throat, making it more comfortable to swallow and alleviating symptoms of strep throat such as soreness and redness.

Buds: Salvia officinalis buds are rich in flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have antimicrobial properties that can help combat bacterial infections that cause strep throat.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 25-30 grams of fresh salvia officinalis leaves and flowers for the tea.
Dry the harvested leaves in a warm oven at 35-40 degrees celsius for 2 hours.
Steep 5 grams of dried leaves in 250 milliliters of boiling water for 5-10 minutes.
Strain the tea and discard the solids after the steeping time has elapsed.
Store the tea in an airtight container in a cool dark place for up to 24 hours.

remedy_recipe: Harvest stevia rebaudiana leaves from mature plants in the morning after the dew has evaporated.

Strain the tea through a fine-mesh sieve into a cup to remove the leaves and solids.
Serve the stevia rebaudiana tea immediately and store any leftover tea in the refrigerator for up to two days.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 to 2 ounces of fresh leaves from the plant when it is in full bloom to ensure optimal flavor and potency.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Rosemary teas helps with strep throat because its antibacterial properties can aid in fighting off the infection. The tea's active compounds, such as carnosic acid, have been shown to inhibit the growth of Streptococcus pyogenes bacteria, which causes strep throat. Additionally, rosemary's anti-inflammatory properties may help reduce swelling and pain in the throat, providing relief from discomfort. As a natural and soothing remedy, rosemary tea can be an effective way to alleviate symptoms and promote recovery.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Rosemary tea's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as camphor and borneol, can help reduce swelling and pain in the throat, alleviating symptoms of strep throat.

Antimicrobial properties: The antibacterial and antiviral compounds present in rosemary tea, including rosmarinic acid and flavonoids, can help combat streptococcus pyogenes bacteria that cause strep throat infections, reducing the severity and duration of the infection.

Decongestant properties: Rosemary tea's decongestant properties can help relieve congestion and reduce swelling in the nasal passages and sinuses, making it easier to breathe and swallow while recovering from strep throat.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rosemary roots have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce swelling in the throat and relieve pain associated with strep throat.
Flowers: Rosemary flowers contain antioxidants and flavonoids that can help combat bacterial infections like strep throat by promoting healthy immune function.
remedy_recipe: Gather one teaspoon of dried rosemary leaves for every eight ounces of boiling water needed.
Combine the dried rosemary leaves with the boiling water in a heat-resistant teapot or cup.
Steep the mixture for at least five minutes and up to ten minutes for optimal potency.
Strain the tea into a clean cup using a tea strainer or cheesecloth to remove solids.
Drink the cooled tea three times a day as needed, with a total daily dose of two to four teaspoons.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Thyme teas helps with strep throat because of its natural antibacterial properties, which effectively combat the infection-causing bacteria. The essential oils present in thyme, such as thymol and carvacrol, possess potent antimicrobial activity that can help reduce the severity and duration of symptoms like sore throats, swollen lymph nodes, and fever. Additionally, thyme's anti-inflammatory compounds may soothe and calm irritated mucous membranes, promoting a smoother recovery from strep throat. By incorporating herbal thyme teas into one's daily routine, individuals may experience relief from these uncomfortable symptoms.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: Thyme's antimicrobial compounds, such as thymol and carvacrol, help combat bacteria like Streptococcus pyogenes, the cause of strep throat infections.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Thyme's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as flavonoids and terpenes, reduce swelling and pain in the throat and tonsils, providing relief from soreness and discomfort.
Soothing and calming properties: Thyme's demulcent and expectorant properties help to calm irritated mucous membranes, thin mucus, and ease coughing, making it easier to breathe and swallow.
remedy_parts: Roots: Thyme roots are used to create a tea that helps to reduce inflammation and soothe sore throats associated with strep throat.
Buds: Thyme buds are a good source of antioxidants and flavonoids, which can help to combat bacterial infections like strep throat and promote overall immune health.
remedy_recipe: Harvest one tablespoon of fresh thyme leaves from your garden or store-bought packet containing 10-15% thymus vulgaris extract.
Boil two cups of water in a medium-sized pot and let it simmer for 5-7 minutes to achieve optimal temperature for herbal infusion.
Add the harvested thyme leaves to the boiling water and let it steep for 5-7 minutes allowing essential oils to release and infuse into the liquid.
Strain the tea mixture through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a large cup or teapot removing any solids and excess thyme leaves.
Let the tea cool down for 1-2 minutes before consuming it, enjoying the soothing aroma and benefits of thymus vulgaris extract in relieving strep throat symptoms.

remedy_recipe: Harvest and rinse 1 cup of fresh or dried vaccinium macrocarpon leaves and stems with cold running water.
Combine the rinsed ingredients with 2 cups of boiling water in a medium-sized pot for 5-7 minutes steeping time.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a teapot or large mug to remove solids.
Add 1 tablespoon of honey and stir until dissolved before serving hot with lemon slices if desired.
Allow the tea to steep for an additional 10-15 minutes after straining to allow maximum extraction of beneficial compounds.

remedy_recipe: Gather one teaspoon of dried prunella vulgaris leaves for every eight ounces of water.
Combine the dried herb with the water in a medium-sized pot and bring it to a boil.
Reduce heat and let it simmer for 10-15 minutes or until the tea reaches a comfortable drinking temperature.
Strain the liquid into a cup using a fine-mesh strainer or cheesecloth to remove any remaining solids.
Drink one cup of the tea three times daily, taking note of any improvements in symptoms within two weeks.

remedy_recipe: Harvest one cup of fresh plantago major leaves and flowers and rinse them gently with cold water.

supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to strep throat that are treated with herbal teas are pharyngitis, tonsillitis, and laryngitis. Herbal teas such as slippery elm, licorice root, and marshmallow root soothe sore throats and reduce inflammation. Yerba mate tea has antimicrobial properties that help combat bacterial infections, while eucalyptus and peppermint teas provide decongestant relief for congested sinuses and a scratchy throat. These herbal teas offer a natural and comforting way to alleviate symptoms and promote healing.
intro_desc: Herbal teas for strep throat are natural remedies that help alleviate symptoms such as sore throat, fever, and inflammation associated with strep throat. They work by boosting the immune system and reducing inflammation. The best herbal teas for strep throat include Echinacea purpurea, Glycyrrhiza glabra, Sambucus nigra, Zingiber officinale, Ulmus rubra, Echinacea angustifolia, Achillea millefolium, Thymus vulgaris, Althaea officinalis, and Eucalyptus globulus. If left untreated, strep throat can lead to missed work or school, sleep deprivation, and chronic illness, negatively impacting overall quality of life and productivity.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal teas that help with strep throat is a blend of slippery elm, echinacea, and peppermint. Slippery elm soothes the throat and reduces inflammation, while echinacea boosts the immune system to fight off the infection. Peppermint provides relief from pain and congestion, and its antimicrobial properties help combat the bacteria causing the infection. This combination helps alleviate symptoms, reduces the duration of the illness, and promotes overall healing.
intro_study: For example, according to a study published by Frontiers in pharmacology, the results showed that patients with chronic pharyngitis who used botanical lozenges made from medicinal plant extracts experienced significant improvement in their pharyngeal symptoms and signs. The total score of pharyngeal symptoms in the experimental group was 3.33 ± 2.33, which was significantly lower than the control group's score of 5.20 ± 2.93 (p < 0.01). By consuming these herbal teas, patients not only recovered from chronic pharyngitis but also had better illness perception and adherence to treatment, making them a good option for managing strep throat.
